_comment: Per-deploy metric-acceptance battery — reconcile-to-raw + chat-vs-MCP parity + governance. Extends verify-governed-metric-gate.sh (numeric/build-stamp) with: (a) reconcile_to = a second independent read that must equal summary.value; (b) parity = the SAME metric over the chat door == the MCP door (identical numbers both doors); (c) governance (data-only-over-MCP, empty-slice-graceful). 'Would have caught the iter-7 regression at build time.' Source: Desktop artifacts/acceptance_battery.json, reconciled to HEAD tool-existence (S3 2026-07-11).
_status: cases with reconcile_to.pending_tool run their self-reconcile + parity legs now; the reconcile-to-raw leg is SKIPPED (loud, tracked) until the named arthik raw-read tool ships — see Q-S3-B5. Never fabricate a provider.

acceptance_cases.assert_variance: summary.variance == summary.value - summary.target (when a target is declared)

acceptance_cases.note: stale-deploy detector; already enforced by verify-governed-metric-gate.sh --expected-sha — mirrored here for battery completeness

acceptance_cases.assert_governance: ui_blocks == [] over the MCP door (render is the client's job)
success == true AND meta.empty == true (no crash on zero rows)
acceptance_cases.note: zero-data over a DECLARED dimension (status) with a value matching nothing must be graceful; uses a real dimension so it exercises the empty-slice path, not the undeclared-dimension teaching-error path
